===================================================================
--- 
+++ 
@@ -1,504 +1,110 @@
-You are a senior Cloudflare Workers and TypeScript architect.
 
-I already have a complete React + Vite frontend UI for Opendrap DevOps AI. The frontend is currently static. Your task is to convert the platform into a fully dynamic SaaS application using Cloudflare's ecosystem.
+ROLE
+You are a senior Django/Python performance & architecture auditor. Read the
+ACTUAL code (never guess), and produce (a) detailed plain-text reports and
+(b) ONE impressive, self-contained interactive HTML dashboard a developer can
+understand at a glance.
 
-IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS
+STEP 0 — ASK ME FIRST (do not start analysing until I answer)
+Ask me these questions and wait for my reply:
+  1. Which app(s) / folder(s) should I analyse? (e.g. the whole repo, or one app)
+  2. Which MODELS should I check in detail? (list them, or say "all models")
+  3. Which view/API FUNCTIONS or modules matter most? (or "all of them")
+  4. What should I focus on? (pick any: performance & DB queries / time per
+     request & concurrency / security / a specific business module like salary
+     or billing / dead code & dependencies / full architecture audit)
+  5. Output: keep BOTH .txt files and the HTML? (default: yes, both)
+If I don't answer some, use sensible defaults and say which you assumed.
 
-* Use TypeScript everywhere.
-* Backend must use Cloudflare Workers.
-* Database must use Cloudflare D1.
-* Realtime communication must use Durable Objects and WebSockets.
-* Storage must support Backblaze B2 integration.
-* Authentication must support Email, Google, and GitHub.
-* Architecture must be production-ready.
-* Follow clean architecture and scalable folder structure.
-* Generate complete code, APIs, database schema, services, repositories, and examples.
+STEP 1 — READ THE CODE (evidence-based, no guessing)
+- List the app's files and line counts. Inventory every model (fields, types,
+  choices, null/blank, FK/M2M relationships + related_names, model methods).
+- Inventory every view/API function/class with its file:line.
+- Trace the request flow and the call chain for the important functions.
+- For any business module I name (e.g. salary), read its engine end to end and
+  document every rule/branch.
+- Confirm the real infrastructure from settings (DB engine, server/ASGI/WSGI,
+  cache, storage, external services). State it explicitly — it changes timing.
 
-TECH STACK
+STEP 2 — ANALYSE (what to look for)
+For each function, report file:line, what it does, approx DB QUERIES, approx
+TIME, and a SEVERITY (HIGH = blocks a worker for seconds or scales linearly;
+MEDIUM = grows with data but bounded; LOW = minor). Specifically hunt for:
+  • Per-group COUNT loops  (for x in xs: qs.count())  -> grouped annotate()
+  • N+1 queries (loop touches obj.rel.rel without select_related/prefetch)
+  • "Is present/exists per row" queries -> fetch IDs once into a set
+  • Synchronous heavy work on the request thread (ML/face inference, Excel/PDF
+    generation, pandas read_excel, image processing, external HTTP/FCM per item)
+  • Loading full querysets then looping in Python instead of DB aggregation
+  • Missing pagination on large lists
+  • Repeated identical queries; df.iterrows() (slowest pandas)
+For timing & concurrency questions: give a per-request step breakdown with
+estimated seconds, identify the dominant cost, then model what happens at
+10/50/100 concurrent requests against the real DB/CPU/GIL/locks. Show the
+formula and be honest these are engineering estimates; include how to MEASURE
+the real numbers (timing logs + a load-test command).
+For salary/business modules: document input/output, every rule and threshold,
+the models read, the call chain, query count & time, and a worked example.
 
-Frontend:
+STEP 3 — WRITE THE .txt REPORTS
+Plain ASCII, fixed-width, section banners with ===, ranked worst-first. One
+file per topic/app, e.g.:
+  PERFORMANCE_ANALYSIS_REPORT.txt   (findings, root causes, action plan)
+  <APP>_ANALYSIS.txt                (per app: models, functions, hotspots)
+  <MODULE>_FULL.txt                 (full field + rule reference if I asked)
+Each finding: File -> Function (line) | Problem | Severity | Fix. Start every
+report with a "root causes" summary and end with a prioritised action plan.
 
-* React
-* Vite
-* TypeScript
-* Tailwind
-* shadcn/ui
+STEP 4 — BUILD ONE IMPRESSIVE HTML (PROJECT_REPORTS.html)
+A SINGLE self-contained file (inline CSS + JS, NO external libraries, no internet
+needed). Make it visual and easy for a non-expert developer to grasp. Required:
 
-Backend:
+  THEME & FEEL
+   - Modern dark UI: deep navy background, vibrant gradient accent
+     (purple -> pink -> orange), glassmorphism cards, soft shadows.
+   - System font stack; monospace for code/file:line; rounded corners; spacing.
+   - Subtle reveal-on-scroll animations (IntersectionObserver or scroll handler)
+     and count-up numbers for hero stats.
 
-* Cloudflare Workers
-* TypeScript
+  STRUCTURE
+   - Sticky top nav with a tab per report/app + a live SEARCH box that filters
+     all finding cards across tabs (auto-expand matches).
+   - One "Overview" tab: animated hero stats + a card per report linking to its
+     tab + a "top fixes" timeline.
+   - One tab per app/topic.
 
-Database:
+  COMPONENTS TO INCLUDE (reuse as relevant)
+   - "In plain English" callout boxes that explain each section simply.
+   - Finding cards: colored severity rail + id + title + file:line + badge,
+     click to expand into "The problem" and a green "The fix" box.
+   - Severity FILTER chips (All / High / Medium / Low) with counts.
+   - For timing: a horizontal WATERFALL bar chart (each step proportional) and
+     a before/after code comparison for the key fix.
+   - For concurrency: a LIVE simulator — sliders for cores & people, a toggle
+     for the fix on/off, outputs (throughput, drain time, last-person wait) that
+     recolor green/amber/red, and an animated grid of dots "draining" the queue.
+   - For an app overview: animated "files by size" bar chart, a models table,
+     and collapsible <details> groups of functions with HIGH/MED/OK badges.
+   - For an audit/scorecard: animated circular SVG gauges.
+   - For a business module: a phase timeline, a rule table with colored value
+     pills, and a worked-example "receipt" card that sums to the result.
+   - A back-to-top button.
 
-* Cloudflare D1
+  QUALITY BARS
+   - Keep all data in small JS arrays at the bottom and render with template
+     strings, so it's easy to extend.
+   - Every claim references a real file:line from the code (no invented numbers;
+     mark estimates as estimates).
+   - Mobile-responsive (grids collapse to 1 column under ~720px).
+   - Tab switching triggers the relevant animations (waterfall/gauges/bars).
+   - Verify the final HTML: exactly one </main>, nav tabs match panel ids,
+     no broken markup.
 
-Realtime:
+STEP 5 — REPORT BACK
+Summarise in chat: the worst offenders, the single biggest win, and the file
+names you created. Offer to add another app/module as a new tab.
 
-* Durable Objects
-* WebSockets
-
-Storage:
-
-* Backblaze B2
-
-AI:
-
-* GLM-4.7 Flash
-
-Agent:
-
-* Python Agent
-

-PHASE 3
-

-

-* Google OAuth

-
-Generate:

-POST /auth/login
-POST /auth/logout

-JWT authentication.

-

-PATCH /users/:id
-DELETE /users/:id

commits.name: Commit-0
commits.content:
You are a senior Cloudflare Workers and TypeScript architect.

I already have a complete React + Vite frontend UI for Opendrap DevOps AI. The frontend is currently static. Your task is to convert the platform into a fully dynamic SaaS application using Cloudflare's ecosystem.

IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS

* Use TypeScript everywhere.
* Backend must use Cloudflare Workers.
* Database must use Cloudflare D1.
* Realtime communication must use Durable Objects and WebSockets.
* Storage must support Backblaze B2 integration.
* Authentication must support Email, Google, and GitHub.
* Architecture must be production-ready.
* Follow clean architecture and scalable folder structure.
* Generate complete code, APIs, database schema, services, repositories, and examples.

TECH STACK

Frontend:

* React
* Vite
* TypeScript
* Tailwind
* shadcn/ui

Backend:

* Cloudflare Workers
* TypeScript

Database:

* Cloudflare D1

Realtime:

* Durable Objects
* WebSockets

Storage:

* Backblaze B2

AI:

* GLM-4.7 Flash

Agent:

* Python Agent

---

PHASE 1

Create backend architecture.

Generate:

src/
├── routes/
├── controllers/
├── services/
├── repositories/
├── middleware/
├── utils/
├── websocket/
├── durable-objects/
├── types/
├── validators/
├── ai/
├── auth/
└── index.ts

Create complete project structure.

---

PHASE 2

Create D1 database schema.

Tables:

users
companies
connections
connection_requests
projects
project_members
deployments
agent_sessions
tasks
task_comments
bugs
bug_comments
chats
chat_members
messages
notifications
activity_logs
api_collections
ai_test_runs
error_logs

Generate:

* SQL schema
* indexes
* relationships
* migrations

---

PHASE 3

Authentication system.

Features:

* Register
* Login
* Logout
* Refresh Token
* Email Login
* Google OAuth
* GitHub OAuth

Generate:

POST /auth/register
POST /auth/login
POST /auth/logout
GET /auth/me

JWT authentication.

Create middleware.

---

PHASE 4

User system.

Features:

* User profile
* Update profile
* Upload avatar
* Skills
* Experience
* Company

Endpoints:

GET /users
GET /users/:id
PATCH /users/:id
DELETE /users/:id

---

PHASE 5

Global Search.

Search by:

* Username
* Company
* Skills
* Projects

Endpoint:

GET /search?q=

Return:

Users
Companies
Projects

---

PHASE 6

Connection System.

Features:

* Send Request
* Accept Request
* Reject Request
* Remove Connection

Endpoints:

POST /connections/request
POST /connections/accept
POST /connections/reject
DELETE /connections/:id

---

PHASE 7

Company System.

Features:

* Create company
* Update company
* Join company
* Leave company
* View members

Endpoints:

POST /companies
GET /companies
GET /companies/:id

---

PHASE 8

Project System.

Features:

* Create Project
* Update Project
* Delete Project
* Invite Members
* Remove Members

Endpoints:

POST /projects
GET /projects
GET /projects/:id
PATCH /projects/:id
DELETE /projects/:id

---

PHASE 9

Realtime Chat.

Use Durable Objects.

Features:

* Private chat
* Group chat
* Project chat

WebSocket Architecture:

User
→ Worker
→ Durable Object
→ WebSocket

Generate:

Chat Durable Object
Message Service
Realtime Events

---

PHASE 10

Task Management.

Kanban board.

Statuses:

Todo
In Progress
Testing
Done

Endpoints:

POST /tasks
GET /tasks
PATCH /tasks/:id
DELETE /tasks/:id

---

PHASE 11

Bug Tracking.

Features:

* Create bug
* Assign bug
* Update status
* Upload screenshot
* Upload video

Statuses:

Open
In Progress
Testing
Fixed
Closed

Endpoints:

POST /bugs
GET /bugs
PATCH /bugs/:id

---

PHASE 12

Agent Communication.

Python Agent connects through WebSocket.

Agent functions:

* Heartbeat
* Git Clone
* Git Pull
* Branch Switch
* Run Command
* Stream Logs
* Upload Results

Generate:

Agent Durable Object
Agent Service
Command Queue

Events:

agent_connected
agent_disconnected
log_received
command_completed

---

PHASE 13

Terminal System.

Create browser terminal.

Flow:

Frontend
→ Worker
→ Durable Object
→ Agent

Generate:

Terminal Session Manager
Terminal Events
Command Streaming

---

PHASE 14

Deployments.

Features:

* Repository connection
* Deployment history
* Branch management
* Deployment status

Endpoints:

POST /deployments
GET /deployments

---

PHASE 15

Cloudflare Tunnel Integration.

Store:

* Tunnel URL
* Status
* Last Seen

Endpoints:

POST /tunnel/start
POST /tunnel/stop
GET /tunnel/status

---

PHASE 16

Logs and Monitoring.

Features:

* Live logs
* Search logs
* Filter logs
* Error tracking

Endpoints:

GET /logs
GET /errors

---

PHASE 17

AI Testing.

Integrate GLM-4.7 Flash.

Features:

* Analyze logs
* Analyze screenshots
* Generate bug summary
* Generate test cases

Endpoints:

POST /ai/analyze-log
POST /ai/analyze-bug
POST /ai/generate-tests

---

PHASE 18

Playwright Testing.

Features:

* Run website test
* Capture screenshots
* Record video
* Store reports

Endpoints:

POST /tests/run
GET /tests/:id

---

PHASE 19

Notifications.

Realtime notifications using Durable Objects.

Events:

Connection Request
Task Assigned
Bug Assigned
Project Invite
Deployment Complete
AI Test Complete

---

PHASE 20

Generate complete API documentation.

For every endpoint provide:

* Route
* Method
* Request Body
* Validation
* Response
* Error Handling
* TypeScript Types
* Example Requests

Generate production-ready code and implement one module completely before moving to the next module.
